messages.text: Ops chat is live. Type here and I pick it up within ~5 minutes — questions, redirects, priorities. Replies and my recent actions appear in this panel.
Building v0.26.0: aging/suppression timeouts (cards 24h, LSRs 3h, alert history), in-app changelog, this chat panel. Research agents out surveying comparable ops tools and new data sources.

Shipped v0.26.0: aging timeouts live (cards >24h and LSRs >3h suppress to aged/history layers — toggle 'aged N' in filters or the map layer control; nothing deleted), in-app changelog (tap the version number), and this chat panel. Crons: hourly release cycle at :23 + chat inbox poll every 5 min. 15-item research-verified backlog added to ROADMAP — next up: MRMS rainfall layer, NOAA 5-day inundation polygons.

v0.27.0 shipped (8:35 AM cycle): MRMS rainfall layers added (layer control → Rainfall 1h/24h, or ?rain=24h link — the 24h swath shows exactly which basins took the water). Data: Falls City crest moved UP to ~10 PM TONIGHT at 25.2 ft major (below record); Sutherland Springs easing but NWS shows a ~35.5 bump this morning — holding the card open; Asherton record watch holds for Sat AM; hundreds of livestock reported lost along Pedernales/Cibolo. APIs green, Sonora FF emergency active to 10:15 AM.

Heard you on the UX — doubling down now. Shipped a declutter first strike (v0.28.0): 4 buttons instead of 15 controls, filters/exports tucked behind toggles, mobile legend collapses, cards open map popups. A dedicated UX auditor agent is screenshotting every screen state right now; its findings drive the next cycles at the new 30-min cadence. HEADS UP: second FLASH FLOOD EMERGENCY — Ozona/Crockett Co, PDS wording, until 11:45 AM. Carded at top.

v0.28.0 shipped. Cadence now 30-min (:23/:53). 3 agents running: deep UX audit, OSS/plugin mining, data-integration specs. Caught + fixed a dangerous honest-zero (gauges tile during an NWPS rate-limit). Ozona FF emergency carded (seed-034).

v0.29.0 shipped (UX audit round 1): stale-data warning bar (amber >7.5min / red >15min — no more stale-looking-fresh), status changes need confirm + resolved is terminal, Alerts tab now shows EMERGENCIES FIRST with a red count badge, phone reclaims ~125px (tiles hidden, disclaimer 1-line), tapping a card opens its map popup. All 3 agents reported: 17-item UX audit, OSS borrow-list (offline tiles possible on LAN http!), corrected data specs. 8 vetted live links added to Resources (iSTAT, DriveTexas, SARiverFlood HALT, CSR activation). Next: USGS 224-site gauge layer + clustering, forecast-crest layer.

Done — release cadence doubled again: cycles now every 15 min (:08/:23/:38/:53), chat still polled every 5. Each cycle ships one focused item top-down from the ranked backlogs (UX-audit remainder first, then the new data layers: USGS 224-site gauges + clustering, forecast-crest layer, live shelters). Screenshot-tested before every ship, same invariants.
Cadence: 30-min → 15-min cycles per owner chat directive (9:22 AM).

v0.30.0 (9:42 cycle): gauge markers tappable one-thumbed (32px hit areas), phone map drops non-flooding gauges. GOOD NEWS: Sutherland Springs secondary bump canceled — falling 33.9, fcst 33.1; homes-cutoff avoided for good. Both FF emergencies still active (Sutton 10:15, Ozona 11:45).
